comment.body:
Before you trade:
1) Have a read on the market; 
I give you a breakdown of the lvls and the daily dynamics within a context, but you have to decide what strategy you will aim that day

2) Determine the money you will put into work that day. 
Be very mature with it! Take the SD of your monthly spending vs incomes (real ones, debt, creadit etc adjusted + inflation if you handle 100k+ account), and be sticked to it!

3)  Take notes, and based on your strat, make a plan of the lvls, what you will do (including your daily profit target and loss target). 
Use probability calculus, VaR, Fractional Kelly, etc. I showed you the basics

4) Never ever deviate from the plan! 
The diff between pro and amateur traders is the control of emotions. Crucial, especially bcs you are trading against algos.
But this also means that if the plan doesn't play out, just leave, don't chase, don't force anything. 

5) Never forget why you are doing all of this.
Life matters. Freedom matters. Not luxury and vanity. 
Money is your servant, not the other way around!
Never forget this. Or you are in their pocket!

Thanks Alma! Been thinking a lot about your geo posts and kept asking myself why.. came to the conclusion that US must have the Middle East relationship (OPEC+) in order to fully execute the drill baby drill plan under Trump. There’s probably not another way to introduce that much supply to the market and keep the price of oil over $50/barrel (which I understand is the break even rate for companies in the Permian) when you have the Saudis making profits when the cost is over $5-6/barrel. 

Then there’s the dynamic of Russia which goes back to your post about culture and China being enemy #1. Which makes sense with the history of the Chinese empire and their constant quest for domination. We can’t rely on them for minerals and production - especially if they cause a war with Taiwan via the One China policy. Hence why the TSM plant was built in Arizona - probably the only good thing to come out of the CHIPS act

Then you have the focus on the 10 year yield my by the US.. how does it come down? You squash inflation and have a growing steady economy (boosting the credit rating) making it cheap to borrow funds again when the gov needs to. Credit based economy.. Sustained growth right? 

Would love to hear if you have any thoughts/insights

comment.body:
It sounds like you’re analyzing market dynamics, likely referencing the S&P 500 (SPX) and relative volatility (RV), with a bear trap scenario in play. Here’s a breakdown of what you’re describing:
	•	A “bear trap” suggests a false signal where the market appears to be heading lower, tricking bearish traders into shorting, only for it to reverse or stabilize. You’re noting this is holding as long as volatility (vol) doesn’t spike downward.
	•	SPX being “mean reverted” and “oscillating” implies it’s fluctuating around its average, without a strong directional trend.
	•	RV down 0.04% could indicate a slight drop in relative volatility, possibly between two assets or indices, suggesting a calm or consolidating market for now.
	•	Key levels to watch: 5680 and 5642. These are likely support zones where downside volatility might kick in if breached.
Your mirage vs. oasis comment seems metaphorical—warning against mistaking a temporary market move (mirage) for a genuine shift (oasis). If you’re looking for confirmation or further insight, feel free to clarify what you’d like me to dig into—levels, vol trends, or something else!

comment.body:
We are correcting the move from the ATH to the 5520-low. 
And in correction there are lots of bull/bear traps since one side isn't much stronger than the other. 
To me this looks like an ABC correction that we have done A up and are now in the B-down (that would form the bear-trap). Then probably into eom due to window dressing, but it can come after we start to head up in the C-leg and that will eventually make the whole retrace between 0.5-0.786 from ATH to 5520-low. 
That leg will be the bull-trap and then continue much lower than 5520

VIX1D: 16

Systematic flows leaning supportive

Call Short: 6165 (~39k OI)
Event catalysts stack into Friday (PCE) and April 2

WTD Range: 5695–5787
5-Day Range: 5600–5790
Post-close E-mini Straddle: ~48 pts → 5740 / 5645
AM Straddle: ~38 pts → 5753 / 5677
Put OI Clusters

NTM: 5695–5710 = trap/congestion zone
OTM: 5675 (gap fill), 5660–65, 5625 (premium seller interest)
Call OI Clusters
ITM: 5700
NTM: 5720/5730 stacked
OTM: 5750–5760 (heavy), 5795/5800 (larger)
SPX ROAD MAP – 0DTE
Opening Reference: SPX near 5715
CHOP ZONE: 5690–5730
IF SPX holds 5715 and remains between 5690 and 5730 → expect chop behavior
IF SPX fades toward 5690 but holds → possible support; charm drift stable
IF SPX rises toward 5730 → watch for stall/rejection unless volume confirms
IF inside range into close → charm drift may lean toward 5690
ABOVE CHOP ZONE

IF SPX >5760 → gamma flip at 5765 may slow momentum

BELOW CHOP ZONE

EXTREME SCENARIOS (±60pts from 5715)
IF SPX >5775 → fast move risk toward 5795–5815
IF SPX >5840 → extension to 5875 possible (structural wall)
IF SPX <5640 → acceleration risk toward 5630
IF SPX <5625 → increased vol, structural magnet not defined
RECLAIM LOGIC
IF SPX breaks <5690 then reclaims 5715 → re-engagement toward 5730
IF SPX breaks >5730 then fails 5715 → reversion risk back to chop
IF SPX overshoots 5765 then fades <5730 → failed breakout confirmation
VOLATILITY & POSITIONING INSIGHTS
VIX backwardation persists; vol surface remains reactive
$TDEX fading = reduced hedging demand
Charm tailwind active (JPM long put decay)
IV expected to be compressible after April 2
TRADE STRUCTURING & EXECUTION
Cautiously bullish bias
Longs: Reclaims of 5715, pullback entries 5650–5660
Shorts: 5730 rejection or extensions to 5780/5820 w/o vol confirmation
Avoid initiating trades inside 5690–5730 without edge/reclaim logic
ADDITIONAL CONSIDERATIONS
8:30 AM: Jobless Claims + GDP Price Index
Tariff-related headlines likely to move tape; algos sensitive
Semis weak on China tensions → green shoots = sector tell
Friday PCE + JPM Collar Reset + April 2 = cluster of vol events
Gamma mixed → wide swings probable, reclaim setups > chasing in both directions

comment.body: Options trading basics. If you're trading options, you need to know what factors affect an option's premium, these are the greeks. This isn’t some high-level quant or math stuff, it’s fundamental for every options trader. You gotta know for example that when you sell an option, you're selling gamma, buying theta, and selling vega.. unless it's 0DTE, because then vega is nearly zero. Plus, there are derivatives of these metrics. There are websites like optionseducation with calculators that show a position's exposure. I'm telling you, these are the basics every options trader needs. Without this knowledge, you shouldn't even be messing with options

comment.body:
I'll explain the options Greeks concepts in Alma's post and answer the specific questions about vomma and zomma.
Understanding the Key Concepts
Alma is describing how market maker positioning changes based on where the S&P 500 (SPX) is trading relative to a specific put strike (5565), and how volatility changes affect their hedging needs.
Long vs Short Vomma
How is long/short vomma determined here?
Vomma (or volga) measures how vega changes with respect to volatility. In the context of this post:
Long vomma position: Above the put strike (5565), market makers have long vomma exposure. This means their vega exposure becomes more positive as volatility increases.
Short vomma position: Below the put strike (5565), market makers have short vomma exposure. This means their vega exposure becomes more negative as volatility increases.
The positioning is determined by the overall options structure market makers have on their books around this significant put strike level.
Vomma's Effect on Zomma
How does long/short vomma exposure affect zomma increasing/decreasing?
Zomma measures how gamma changes with respect to volatility. In Alma's explanation:
When market makers are long vomma (above strike), decreasing volatility leads to increasing positive zomma until ~5858-5870 range. This accelerates delta changes, requiring them to buy futures when hedging.
When market makers are short vomma (below strike), decreasing volatility leads to increasing positive zomma until ~5110-5115 range. This also accelerates delta changes, but requires them to sell futures when hedging.
The relationship works because both vomma and zomma are higher-order derivatives related to volatility. When volatility decreases, the rate of change in hedging needs (zomma) increases in the direction of the vomma exposure.
Practical Trading Implications
What Alma is describing is important for understanding potential market moves. As volatility decreases:
If SPX is above 5565: Market makers need to buy more futures as prices rise (potentially accelerating upward moves)
If SPX is below 5565: Market makers need to sell more futures as prices fall (potentially accelerating downward moves)
This creates a "convexity" effect that can amplify market movements in either direction as volatility falls, with specific target ranges where these effects might be strongest.

When market makers are short vomma (below strike), decreasing volatility leads to increasing positive zomma until ~5110-5115 range. This also accelerates delta changes, but requires them to sell futures when hedging.
The relationship works because both vomma and zomma are higher-order derivatives related to volatility. When volatility decreases, the rate of change in hedging needs (zomma) increases in the direction of the vomma exposure.
Practical Trading Implications
What Alma is describing is important for understanding potential market moves. As volatility decreases:
If SPX is above 5565: Market makers need to buy more futures as prices rise (potentially accelerating upward moves)
If SPX is below 5565: Market makers need to sell more futures as prices fall (potentially accelerating downward moves)
This creates a "convexity" effect that can amplify market movements in either direction as volatility falls, with specific target ranges where these effects might be strongest.

comment.body:
Before you trade:
1) Have a read on the market; 
I give you a breakdown of the lvls and the daily dynamics within a context, but you have to decide what strategy you will aim that day

2) Determine the money you will put into work that day. 
Be very mature with it! Take the SD of your monthly spending vs incomes (real ones, debt, creadit etc adjusted + inflation if you handle 100k+ account), and be sticked to it!

3)  Take notes, and based on your strat, make a plan of the lvls, what you will do (including your daily profit target and loss target). 
Use probability calculus, VaR, Fractional Kelly, etc. I showed you the basics

4) Never ever deviate from the plan! 
The diff between pro and amateur traders is the control of emotions. Crucial, especially bcs you are trading against algos.
But this also means that if the plan doesn't play out, just leave, don't chase, don't force anything. 

5) Never forget why you are doing all of this.
Life matters. Freedom matters. Not luxury and vanity. 
Money is your servant, not the other way around!
Never forget this. Or you are in their pocket!

comment.body: An observation:  one point that I understand Alma emphasizes in most posts is for us to look for divergences between the mechanical flows -- what "should happen" based on positioning, etc. -- and what actually happens, the latter revealing the hand of the customer.  Here, we see that RV has been dropping all morning (note the white curve I drew above the RV indicator for emphasis; this is drilled in on a 3min chart).  And even with low local RV, we did not break through the zero vanna line despite repeated tests from above when that low vol should have allowed that to happen.  So if I am understanding her correctly, this confirms her theory.  There is customer buying interest overcoming the mechanics.  Having wrecked some bears with the pop today, perhaps we are basing today to wreck some more bears tomorrow and move into the bull trap phase.  Also, importantly, we are realizing low vol even at these lowish price levels after a nasty drop yesterday, which means increasing liquidity, a primary goal of this market activity before the flush (therefore expect the bull phase to be move up on low vol into positive gamma).  Welcome input or comments on this take.

comment.body:
do you mean this one? 
//@version=5
indicator("GKYZ Volatility", shorttitle="GKYZ Vol. Est.", overlay=false)



lookback = input.int(5, title="Lookback Period", minval=1)  
ma_period=input.int(20,title="MA Period", minval=1)


f_calcDailyRV() =>



    window          = lookback
    trading_periods = 390



    log_hoD = math.log(high / open)
    log_loD = math.log(low  / open)
    log_coD = math.log(close / open)



    log_ocD    = math.log(open / nz(close[1]))
    log_oc_sqD = log_ocD * log_ocD



    log_ccD    = math.log(close / nz(close[1]))
    log_cc_sqD = log_ccD * log_ccD



    rsD = log_hoD*(log_hoD - log_coD) + log_loD*(log_loD - log_coD)



    close_volD = math.sum(log_cc_sqD, window) / (window - 1)
    open_volD  = math.sum(log_oc_sqD, window) / (window - 1)
    window_rsD = math.sum(rsD, window)        / (window - 1)
    kD         = 0.34 / (1.34 + (window + 1.0)/(window - 1.0))
    rvD        = math.sqrt((open_volD + kD*close_volD + (1 - kD)*window_rsD) * trading_periods)



dailyRV = request.security(syminfo.tickerid, "1", f_calcDailyRV())
ma=ta.sma(dailyRV,ma_period)
plot(dailyRV, title="GKYZ Volatility", color=#970ef3, linewidth=2)
plot(ma,title="Moving Average", color = #00ff00, linewidth=2)

comment.body: You have to read the gamma and charm maps together, recognizing they reflect dealer mechanics and reaction to price action only, and are not deterministic.  So the orange hues reflect negative gamma, meaning the dealer mechanics will increase the magnitude of the move, up or down.  (In negative gamma, if spot moves up, dealers' delta goes down, so they have to buy more deltas (futures) to stay delta neutral.  If spot goes down, dealers' delta goes up, so they have to sell deltas to stay delta neutral).  The blue on the charm map is negative charm, that is, dealers' delta decays with time, meaning they have to buy deltas to stay delta neutral and that acts as a bit of a tailwind/supportive.  Yellow on the charm map is positive charm, meaning dealers' delta increases with time and they have to sell deltas as time passes and that acts as a headwind/suppressive. So we saw a nice rally assisted by negative gamma despite the suppressive charm and almost made it into the supportive charm when selling interest came in, and then that negative gamma accentuated the down move (dealers selling with the seller).  And this next part is beyond my current understanding but the very faintly shaded area on the gamma map indicates low speed (very little change in gamma with spot) which (I believe) allows volatility to play a more dominant role and we saw RV jump the last 20 min or so.

i advise to always keep the pre-market OD chart in mind, and do not fully rely on the updated charts.
